memes.label: Alca
memes.description: Alca, the capital city of Penguinia — the urban center where most of Penguin Island's political and social satire unfolds.

memes.label: Annals of Penguinia
memes.description: A skeptical history of Penguinia by Ovidius Capito that denies the miracle of the Penguin transformation, representing the rationalist historiographical tradition.

memes.label: Baptism of the Penguins
memes.description: The baptism of penguins by Saint Maël — the founding theological event of Penguin Island and a satirical exploration of sacramental theology.

memes.label: Chatillon
memes.description: The Emiral Chatillon, a handsome and celebrated military commander in Penguinia whose entanglement with the Viscountess Olive draws him into the royalist conspiracy.

memes.label: Church in Penguin Island
memes.description: The institutional Church as a satirical target in Anatole France's Penguin Island — its wealth, political power, and manipulation of popular faith for institutional benefit.

memes.label: Church of Penguinia
memes.description: The Church of Penguinia — the institutional church established by Saint Maël, satirized by Anatole France as a political-economic organization rather than a spiritual community.

memes.label: Civilization in Penguin Island
memes.description: Civilization as a satirical concept in Penguin Island — the rise, peak, and collapse of Penguin society as a commentary on human progress.

memes.label: Dragon of Alca
memes.description: The foundational crisis of early Penguin history — a "dragon" terrorized the villages of Alca, devouring livestock and children, until the hero Kraken slew it. In reality, Kraken WAS the dragon, wearing an elaborate costume to extort the communities he later claimed to have saved.

memes.label: Faith in Penguin Island
memes.description: Faith — religious, political, and superstitious — as a driving force and satirical target in Anatole France's Penguin Island.

memes.label: Father Agaric
memes.description: Father Agaric, the conspiratorial monk who plots to restore the monarchy — Anatole France's satirical portrait of clerical political intrigue.

memes.label: Father Cornemuse
memes.description: A Penguin monk and political conspirator who, alongside Father Agaric, works to restore the monarchy and advance the interests of the Church through intrigue.

memes.label: Gesta Penguinorum
memes.description: The celebrated 12-book Latin chronicle of Penguin history composed by the monk Johannes Talpa at the monastery of Beargarden, the foundational historiographical work of Penguinia.

memes.label: Greatauk
memes.description: General Greatauk, Duke of Skull, the Minister of War of Penguinia and a central antagonist in the Pyrot affair, satirizing the French military establishment.

memes.label: Justice in Penguin Island
memes.description: The satirical portrayal of justice — both divine and human — in Anatole France's Penguin Island, from theological debates in Paradise to the corrupt courts of modern Penguinia.

memes.label: Kraken
memes.description: Legendary founder of the Draconides dynasty, husband of Orberosia, and the original "Dragon of Alca" — a Penguin who used an elaborate monster costume to terrorize villages and accumulate wealth.

memes.label: Miracle Cult in Penguin Island
memes.description: The miracle cult of Saint Orberosia as a satirical exposé of religious commerce — how relics, miracles, and popular credulity built an ecclesiastical economy in Penguin Island.

memes.label: Orberosia
memes.description: The first Penguin woman to wear the garments of chastity, wife of Kraken, lover of Marcel the shepherd and a hunchbacked neatherd, later venerated as Saint Orberosia — a satire of hagiography and the accidental nature of sanctity.

memes.label: Parliament of Penguinia
memes.description: The legislative body of the Penguin nation through all its political forms — from the first assembly of thirty Elders under a sycamore tree to the modern Parliament palace controlled by a financial oligarchy. A sustained satire of representative government.

memes.label: Penguin Island
memes.description: The titular Arctic island — originally inhabited by penguins, towed to the Breton coast by Saint Maël's miraculous stone trough, and the physical foundation of the Penguin nation whose very insularity may be a geographical fiction.

memes.label: Penguinia
memes.description: The island nation of the Penguins — the primary setting of Anatole France's satirical chronicle, a fictional mirror of France that evolves from primitive baptism through medieval monarchy, Renaissance, and modern republicanism over eight books.

memes.label: Prince Crucho
memes.description: The last of the Draconides dynasty and pretender to the Penguin throne, around whom royalist conspirators rally in the modern era.

memes.label: Property in Penguin Island
memes.description: The foundational institution of Penguin civil society — the act of setting bounds to the fields establishes private property as the first law, from which all subsequent legal, political, and social structures descend. France's satire of enclosure, accumulation, and the violence underlying property rights.

memes.label: Pyrot
memes.description: A middle-class Jewish man in Penguinia falsely accused of stealing eighty thousand trusses of hay, the central figure in the Dreyfus Affair satire of Book VI.

memes.label: Religion in Penguin Island
memes.description: Religion as a central satirical target in Anatole France's Penguin Island — a critique of institutional faith, clerical politics, and the intertwining of church and state.

memes.label: Revolution in Penguin Island
memes.description: Revolution as a recurring political pattern in Anatole France's Penguin Island — its causes, rhetoric, and inevitable failure to transform society.

memes.label: Saint Maël
memes.description: Saint Maël, the missionary who baptizes the penguins — a holy fool whose accidental act founds Penguin civilization and its religious tradition.

memes.label: The Pyrot Affair
memes.description: The Pyrot Affair — Anatole France's satirical allegory of the Dreyfus Affair, in which an innocent penguin is convicted of stealing eighty thousand trusses of hay.

memes.label: Virtue in Penguin Island
memes.description: Virtue — moral, civic, and martial — as a contested ideal in Anatole France's Penguin Island, where claims of virtue often mask vice, ambition, or self-deception.
